name: Septizonium
note: Built by Septimius Severus, dedicated 203 AD; no P571 on Wikidata and the Pleiades description has no parseable date.

name: Arch of Gallienus
note: Rededicated to Gallienus 262 AD (Augustan gate beneath it is not this record's identity); no P571.

name: Nymphaeum divi Alexandri
note: Nymphaeum of Alexander Severus, ~226 AD; no P571.

name: Torre delle Milizie
note: Medieval tower (early 13th c.) — the Pleiades span (-200..500) belongs to remains beneath it, but the record's identity is the tower.

name: Torre dei Capocci
note: Medieval tower (12th c.); same rebuild-identity class as Torre delle Milizie.

name: Santi Giovanni e Paolo
note: Basilica founded late 4th c. (398); the -337 on the religious sibling is a conquest floor and Wikidata's P571 (1951) is the modern rededication. The id key reaches the qid-less religious record.

name: Circus Varianus
note: Circus at the Sessorium, begun under Caracalla ~212 AD; no P571.

name: Villa Pamphili
note: Pleiades findspot in the Villa Doria Pamphilj gardens — the ancient villa/necropolis dates ~50-400 AD (religion-pleiades agrees); the -30..1453 span was a period floor. The modern name is a separate curated-naming question.

name: Thermae Helenianae
note: Bath complex of the Horti Variani, Severan (~220s); renamed for Helena after her ~326 restoration. No P571; the -30 was a period floor.

name: Odeon (Little Theatre of Pompeii)
note: Built ~80 BC in the Sullan colony; the stamped 81 AD start is a Flavian period floor — impossible, Pompeii was buried in 79. The Vesuvius event cap supplies the end.

name: Dura-Europos house church
note: Converted to a church ~232-235; the stamped 300 is a period floor and impossible — the city was destroyed in 256/7. The Dura event cap supplies the end.

name: Ponte Sisto
note: Sixtus IV's bridge (1473) — post-window, so never rendered. The ancient Pons Aurelius under it would need its own record.
